title: Ctrl_Unvac_3
extract_protocol_ch1: Approximately 2.5mL of venous blood were collect from all participants using Paxgene RNA collection scheme (PreAnalytiX, Hombrechtikon, Switzerland). Further, the total RNA was isolated by PAXgene Blood miRNA Kit (PreAnalytiX, Hombrechtikon, Switzerland) which is indicated for the isolation and purification of the total RNA longer than 18 nucleotides (nt) from the whole blood collected with the Paxgene tubes. The manufacturer’s instructions were strictly followed at both blood collection and RNA purification stages.
extract_protocol_ch1.1: The samples were submitted to a previous mRNA isolation step by Magnetic mRNA isolation kit. Which is designed to isolate intact polyadenosine (poly A) RNA. Then, the purified mRNA was used as input for the sequencing library preparation using the ScriptSeqTM V2 RNA-Seq library preparation kit (Epicenter) as per the manufacturer’s instructions.Subsequently, the sequencing was performed following the standard protocols on Illumina HiSeq® 2500 Sequencing System (Illumina, San Diego, CA) running 100 bp paired-end reads (PE100) and generating approximately 30 million reads passing filter for each sample to produce the mRNA reads.
data_processing: The below briefly described steps for library preparation, sequencing, pre-processing and mapping, were performed at the Genome Analytics facilities of the Helmholtz Zentrum für Infektionsforschung (Braunschweiger/Germany).
data_processing.1: The sequencing was performed following the standard protocols on Illumina HiSeq® 2500 Sequencing System (Illumina, San Diego, CA) running 100 bp paired-end reads (PE100) and generating approximately 30 million reads passing filter for each sample to produce the mRNA reads.
data_processing.2: Sequenced reads were trimmed for adaptor sequence using TrimGalore with the trimming mode as paired-end, and masked (hemoglobins, rRNA, tRNA and chromossome M mRNAs) for low-complexity or low-quality sequence, then mapped to hg19 whole genome using bowtie2.
data_processing.3: After mapping, the aligned transcripts were assembled using Cufflinks (v2.1.1) standard parameters. To calculate the expression values and test the statistical difference in the mRNA expression we used the Cuffdiff tool.
data_processing.4: Venn diagram analysis was performed to identify mRNAs differently expressed (q < 0.01, p < 0.05) in the blood of TB patients compared to both control and LTBI groups. Then, AUROC (area under the receiver operating characteristics curve) analysis was used for the selection of target-gene candidates
data_processing.6: Supplementary_files_format_and_content: Expresison data matrix file with the normalised expression value of each gene (ENSEMBL)
